title: Self-DD intra-module cross-layer reads
summary: Let layers inside a module read earlier layer outputs in addition to the immediately preceding state.
hypothesis: Learned access to earlier layer states can improve information reuse beyond a strictly sequential residual stack.
design: Use V21SelfDD and V21DepthDD to select and mix same-module history states independently of cross-module residual routes.

delta.summary: Let layers inside a module read earlier layer outputs in addition to the immediately preceding state.

operations.before: each layer consumes only the immediately preceding hidden state
operations.after: V21SelfDD/V21DepthDD read and mix stacked earlier outputs within the same module
operations.rationale: Same-module history reads are independently switchable and are the direct parent of D09.

commits.summary: Shared aggregated history starting point; not a unique introduction commit for this Feature.

analysis.conclusion: Self-DD is independently configured in code, but its isolated effect and the exact expansion of DD require algorithm-owner confirmation.
limitations: No isolated parent-relative ablation artifact is available.
The initial commit is shared with five other reviewed base mechanisms.

evidence.summary: The first available repository commit is an aggregated ConceptLM V2/V2.1/V2.2-VQ snapshot; it supports six independently reviewed structures without exposing separate introduction commits or ablation results.

work-repo.note: Derived private work-repository evidence; never root or official OLMo-3 provenance.

summary_zh: 在模块内部加入跨层读取：每层除接收上一层输出外，还可读取此前所有层输出；初始实现保存并堆叠完整层历史。

validation.comparison: Strictly sequential intra-module layers versus learned reads over earlier same-module layer states.
conditions: Retrospective code inspection at the shared initial snapshot a489526d1dff4161a60dccc5034c2d595f059d49.
No isolated parent-relative result artifact is available.
observations: Self-DD is independently configured in code, but its isolated effect and the exact expansion of DD require algorithm-owner confirmation.
validation.conclusion: Self-DD is independently configured in code, but its isolated effect and the exact expansion of DD require algorithm-owner confirmation.
limitations: No isolated parent-relative ablation artifact is available.
The initial commit is shared with five other reviewed base mechanisms.
